exemplar.title: 23.  How important are material tools in the production or acquisition of knowledge?

detailedExemplarData.title: 23.  How important are material tools in the production or acquisition of knowledge?

strengths: Precise identification of three distinct real-world objects with clear historical and contextual grounding
Consistent, explicit links to the IA prompt with detailed discussion of how each material tool enables and constrains knowledge
Robust use of evidence and comparisons (e.g., Van Gogh, ribosome dimensions, source citations) to support claims and justifications
weaknesses: Occasional lapses in precision (e.g., minor descriptive repetition in the conclusion and a few unsubstantiated assertions)
Errors and inconsistencies in citation formatting (dead links, incomplete URLs, typographical mistakes)
Minor factual inaccuracies (misstated nanoscale for mitochondria, misspelling of âFrida Kahloâ) that slightly undermine scientific and artistic credibility
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The exhibition fulfills and exceeds the 7â8 band by providing clear identification of three well-contextualized objects and by making strong, well-supported links to the IA prompt. The detailed evidence, individualised justifications, and repeated explicit references place the work firmly in the 9â10 band. However, minor stylistic, factual, and citation errors prevent a flawless performance, warranting the lower mark within the top band. Thus, a final score of 9 accurately reflects the exhibitionâs overall excellence tempered by these small but noteworthy weaknesses.

annotations.comment: The electron-microscope image is well-contextualized from a recognized IB Biology source, strengthening the objectâs real-world relevance.
Object 2âs title clearly states the tool, source, and date, contributing to precise identification and context.
Relying on Goodreads for Mozartâs quotations may undermine reliability; seek primary musicological sources or critical editions for accuracy.
The student begins with a precise restatement of the IA prompt, grounding the exhibition clearly in the TOK question from the outset.
Object 1 is clearly identified with title, artist, and date, demonstrating awareness of its real-world context.
Typographical error: the artistâs name is mistyped as âFriday Kahloâ rather than Frida Kahlo. Correcting such errors enhances credibility.
The discussion would benefit from explicit terminology referencing âmaterial toolsâ when describing paintbrush, paints, and canvas to reinforce the link to the IA prompt.
The comparison to Van Goghâs âStarry Nightâ is descriptive but lacks depth. Explore more nuanced parallels to how different material tools shape distinct artistic knowledge claims.
The concluding claim asserts that knowledge production depends solely on the creator, but the prompt asks about the importance of tools. Consider a more balanced view of tool-knower interaction.
The claim of âcomplete certaintyâ in photographic evidence overlooks the provisional nature of scientific knowledge. Introduce nuance regarding interpretative elements.
The stated nanoscale of â0.5â1 nanometersâ for mitochondria is inaccurate (should be micrometers). Verify figures to maintain scientific rigor.
The concluding paragraph repeats earlier points. Streamline this section and tie each sentence back explicitly to the prompt.
Integrate the term âmaterial toolâ when first describing the image to foreground TOK terminology and reinforce the central prompt.
The recovered Mozart manuscript choice is compelling and authentic, strengthening the exhibitionâs diversity of tools across domains.
The manuscript is situated in its specific historical period (1785) and linked to a modern performance, giving clear real-world context.
Expand on how Mozartâs quote informs the role of silence as part of the material toolâs limitations and affordances.
Insightful observation that the knowerâs expertise shapes interpretationâthe link between pre-existing knowledge and the toolâs usability is well made.
Consider citing a study on historical performance practice to illustrate how additional tools (treatises, recordings) influence interpretation.
The word count appears in the main text. Administrative details should be removed from the exhibition itself.
The Works Cited header is clearly labeled, indicating good structural organization of sources.
The Ergo Magazine citation omits italics for the publication name and lacks consistent punctuation. Apply a uniform citation style.
Include access dates for online encyclopedia entries (e.g., Britannica) to meet standard citation requirements.
The Science Photo Library URL appears incomplete; please verify and ensure that the reader can access the original image source.
The cited Museo Frida Kahlo page shows âPÃ¡gina No Encontrada,â indicating a dead link. Replace with a valid source or archive.
Ensure consistency by listing the author or institution before the title in each citation, as in the âTree of Hopeâ entry.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content:
The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA

these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not

There is very little
justification offered for

The commentary on the
objects is highly

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
